questions.question: Substances exist as solids, liquids or gases. In which states of matter are the particles a similar distance apart? A solid, liquid and gas, B solid and liquid only, C solid and gas only, D liquid and gas only
questions.text: Substances exist as solids, liquids or gases. In which states of matter are the particles a similar distance apart? A solid, liquid and gas, B solid and liquid only, C solid and gas only, D liquid and gas only

questions.question: A container with a fixed shape is filled with gas and then sealed. The container is then heated. Which changes take place? 1   The volume of gas decreases. 2   The gas molecules move faster.
questions.text: A container with a fixed shape is filled with gas and then sealed. The container is then heated. Which changes take place? 1   The volume of gas decreases. 2   The gas molecules move faster.

questions.question: The pressure inside the cylinder increases. A 1 and 2 B   1 only C   2 and 3 D   3 only 3   A crystal of sodium chloride is dropped into a beaker of water. Over time, the sodium chloride particles become evenly distributed throughout the water. Which processes occur in this experiment?, A both dissolving and diffusion, B dissolving only, C diffusion only, D neither dissolving nor diffusion 3

questions.question: The electronic structures of two atoms, P and Q, are shown. QP P and Q combine together to form a compound. What is the type of bonding in the compound, and what is the formula of the compound? type of bonding formula A covalent PQ 2, B covalent PQ, C ionic PQ, D ionic PQ 2
questions.text: The electronic structures of two atoms, P and Q, are shown. QP P and Q combine together to form a compound. What is the type of bonding in the compound, and what is the formula of the compound? type of bonding formula A covalent PQ 2, B covalent PQ, C ionic PQ, D ionic PQ 2

questions.question: The table shows the nucleon number and the number of neutrons in one atom of isotopes W, X, Y and Z. isotope nucleon number number of neutrons W 35 18 X 37 20 Y 39 20 Z 40 22 Which statement about W, X, Y and Z is correct? A W and X are isotopes of the same element., B X and Y are isotopes of elements in the same group of the Periodic Table., C Y and Z are isotopes of elements in the same period of the Periodic Table., D Z has a higher proton number than Y.
questions.text: The table shows the nucleon number and the number of neutrons in one atom of isotopes W, X, Y and Z. isotope nucleon number number of neutrons W 35 18 X 37 20 Y 39 20 Z 40 22 Which statement about W, X, Y and Z is correct? A W and X are isotopes of the same element., B X and Y are isotopes of elements in the same group of the Periodic Table., C Y and Z are isotopes of elements in the same period of the Periodic Table., D Z has a higher proton number than Y.
options: A W and X are isotopes of the same element.
B X and Y are isotopes of elements in the same group of the Periodic Table.
C Y and Z are isotopes of elements in the same period of the Periodic Table.
D Z has a higher proton number than Y.

questions.question: Which statement about an ionic bond is correct? A It is a strong electrostatic attraction between cations and anions., B It is a strong electrostatic attraction between protons and electrons., C It is a weak electrostatic attraction between cations and anions., D It is a weak electrostatic attraction between protons and electrons.
questions.text: Which statement about an ionic bond is correct? A It is a strong electrostatic attraction between cations and anions., B It is a strong electrostatic attraction between protons and electrons., C It is a weak electrostatic attraction between cations and anions., D It is a weak electrostatic attraction between protons and electrons.
options: A It is a strong electrostatic attraction between cations and anions.
B It is a strong electrostatic attraction between protons and electrons.
C It is a weak electrostatic attraction between cations and anions.
D It is a weak electrostatic attraction between protons and electrons.

questions.text: Which row describes simple molecular compounds? melting point electrical conductivity when molten A high good, B high poor, C low good, D low poor 5

questions.question: Boron nitride is a compound of the elements boron and nitrogen. It has a similar structure to diamond. What is likely to be a property of boron nitride? A It conducts electricity., B It is soluble in water., C It is used as a lubricant., D It is very hard.
questions.text: Boron nitride is a compound of the elements boron and nitrogen. It has a similar structure to diamond. What is likely to be a property of boron nitride? A It conducts electricity., B It is soluble in water., C It is used as a lubricant., D It is very hard.
options: B It is soluble in water.
C It is used as a lubricant.

questions.question: Which statement defines the term molecular formula? A a formula that shows the total number of atoms in a molecule, B a formula that shows the total number of elements in a molecule, C a formula that shows the arrangement of different atoms in a molecule, D a formula that shows the number and type of different atoms in a molecule
questions.text: Which statement defines the term molecular formula? A a formula that shows the total number of atoms in a molecule, B a formula that shows the total number of elements in a molecule, C a formula that shows the arrangement of different atoms in a molecule, D a formula that shows the number and type of different atoms in a molecule
options: A a formula that shows the total number of atoms in a molecule
B a formula that shows the total number of elements in a molecule
C a formula that shows the arrangement of different atoms in a molecule
D a formula that shows the number and type of different atoms in a molecule

questions.question: Which statements explain why metal objects are electroplated? 1   Electroplating improves the appearance of the object. 2   Electroplating insulates the metal object. 3   Electroplating makes the metal object more resistant to corrosion. 4   Electroplating makes the metal object softer. A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C   2 and 4 D   3 and 4
questions.text: Which statements explain why metal objects are electroplated? 1   Electroplating improves the appearance of the object. 2   Electroplating insulates the metal object. 3   Electroplating makes the metal object more resistant to corrosion. 4   Electroplating makes the metal object softer. A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C   2 and 4 D   3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C   2 and 4 D   3 and 4

questions.question: Three statements about a hydrogen–oxygen fuel cell are listed. 1   The overall equation for the reaction is 2H 2 +  O 2   2H 2 O. 2   The hydrogen required comes from the air. 3   The waste gas causes respiratory problems. Which statements are correct? A 1, 2 and 3 B   1 only C   2 and 3 only D   3 only 6
questions.text: Three statements about a hydrogen–oxygen fuel cell are listed. 1   The overall equation for the reaction is 2H 2 +  O 2   2H 2 O. 2   The hydrogen required comes from the air. 3   The waste gas causes respiratory problems. Which statements are correct? A 1, 2 and 3 B   1 only C   2 and 3 only D   3 only 6

questions.question: Some physical and chemical changes are listed. 1   rusting of iron 2   condensing of steam 3   dissolving of sugar in water 4   cracking of alkanes Which changes are physical changes? A 1 and 3 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   2 and 4

options: A 1 and 3 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   2 and 4

questions.question: Blue copper( II) sulfate crystals are heated in air until they turn into a white powder. The powder is allowed to cool and after a few days it starts to turn blue. Why does the white powder start to turn blue? A Carbon dioxide in the air reacts with the powder to form copper( II) carbonate., B The powder reacts with water in the air to form copper( II) hydroxide., C The white copper compound is slowly oxidised., D Water is absorbed from the air and causes the reaction to reverse. 7
questions.text: Blue copper( II) sulfate crystals are heated in air until they turn into a white powder. The powder is allowed to cool and after a few days it starts to turn blue. Why does the white powder start to turn blue? A Carbon dioxide in the air reacts with the powder to form copper( II) carbonate., B The powder reacts with water in the air to form copper( II) hydroxide., C The white copper compound is slowly oxidised., D Water is absorbed from the air and causes the reaction to reverse. 7
options: A Carbon dioxide in the air reacts with the powder to form copper( II) carbonate.
B The powder reacts with water in the air to form copper( II) hydroxide.
C The white copper compound is slowly oxidised.
D Water is absorbed from the air and causes the reaction to reverse. 7

questions.text: Carbon reacts with silver oxide to form carbon dioxide and silver. Which substance is reduced? A carbon, B carbon dioxide, C silver, D silver oxide

Which row  gives  the  colour  of  thymolphthalein  in  aqueous Q  and the  ion  responsible for this colour? colour of thymolphthalein ion A blue H +, B colourless H +, C blue OH –, D colourless OH –

questions.question: Elements L and M are in the same group of the Periodic Table. Why do L and M have similar chemical properties? A They have the same number of electrons in the nucleus., B They have the same number of electrons in the outer shell., C They have the same number of neutrons in the nucleus., D They have the same number of neutrons in the outer shell. 8
questions.text: Elements L and M are in the same group of the Periodic Table. Why do L and M have similar chemical properties? A They have the same number of electrons in the nucleus., B They have the same number of electrons in the outer shell., C They have the same number of neutrons in the nucleus., D They have the same number of neutrons in the outer shell. 8
options: A They have the same number of electrons in the nucleus.
B They have the same number of electrons in the outer shell.
C They have the same number of neutrons in the nucleus.
D They have the same number of neutrons in the outer shell. 8

questions.question: The diagram shows elements W, X, Y and Z in a section of the Periodic Table. X Y Z W Which statement about the reactivity of the elements is correct? A X is more reactive than Y, and W is more reactive than Z., B X is more reactive than Y, and Z is more reactive than W., C Y is more reactive than X, and W is more reactive than Z., D Y is more reactive than X, and Z is more reactive than W.
questions.text: The diagram shows elements W, X, Y and Z in a section of the Periodic Table. X Y Z W Which statement about the reactivity of the elements is correct? A X is more reactive than Y, and W is more reactive than Z., B X is more reactive than Y, and Z is more reactive than W., C Y is more reactive than X, and W is more reactive than Z., D Y is more reactive than X, and Z is more reactive than W.
options: A X is more reactive than Y, and W is more reactive than Z.
B X is more reactive than Y, and Z is more reactive than W.
C Y is more reactive than X, and W is more reactive than Z.
D Y is more reactive than X, and Z is more reactive than W.

B barium sulfate, calcium chloride and magnesium hydroxide

questions.question: 18 carat gold contains 75% gold and 25% copper by mass. Which statements about 18 carat gold are correct? 1   It is a mixture. 2   It is a compound. 3   It is an alloy. 4   It conducts electricity. A 1, 2 and 4 B   1, 3 and 4 C   1 and 3 only D   2 and 3 9
questions.text: 18 carat gold contains 75% gold and 25% copper by mass. Which statements about 18 carat gold are correct? 1   It is a mixture. 2   It is a compound. 3   It is an alloy. 4   It conducts electricity. A 1, 2 and 4 B   1, 3 and 4 C   1 and 3 only D   2 and 3 9
options: A 1, 2 and 4 B   1, 3 and 4 C   1 and 3 only D   2 and 3 9

questions.question: The properties of four metals, E, F, G and H, are shown. E does not react with cold water, but reacts with steam. F does not react with water or dilute acid, but the oxide of F is reduced by carbon. The oxide of G is not reduced by carbon, but G reacts vigorously with cold water. H does not react with water or steam, but reacts with dilute acid. What is the order of reactivity of the metals starting with the most reactive? most reactive least reactive A F E H G, B F H E G, C G E H F, D G H E F
questions.text: The properties of four metals, E, F, G and H, are shown. E does not react with cold water, but reacts with steam. H does not react with water or steam, but reacts with dilute acid. What is the order of reactivity of the metals starting with the most reactive? most reactive least reactive A F E H G, B F H E G, C G E H F, D G H E F

A 1 and 3 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   2 and 4

questions.question: Which statement about alcohols is correct? A Alcohols have the same functional group as carboxylic acids., B Ethanol is an alcohol that is formed by fermentation at 300 °C., C Ethanol is the alcohol produced in the addition reaction between ethene and hydrogen., D Water is produced from the combustion of alcohols. 12
questions.text: Which statement about alcohols is correct? A Alcohols have the same functional group as carboxylic acids., B Ethanol is an alcohol that is formed by fermentation at 300 °C., C Ethanol is the alcohol produced in the addition reaction between ethene and hydrogen., D Water is produced from the combustion of alcohols. 12
options: A Alcohols have the same functional group as carboxylic acids.
B Ethanol is an alcohol that is formed by fermentation at 300 °C.
C Ethanol is the alcohol produced in the addition reaction between ethene and hydrogen.
D Water is produced from the combustion of alcohols. 12

questions.question: A student uses paper chromatography to identify the food dyes in a coloured sweet, S. The student uses four known food dyes, W, X, Y, and Z, and ethanol as the solvent. A 1, 2 and 4 B   2 and 3 C   2 and 4 only D   4 only 14
